friction_items.description: A fresh MCP client connecting to research-os ships back ~200 distinct tools (203 unique keys in TOOL_DEFINITIONS; 344 registered entries counting pack tools + aliases) on the very first list_tools() call. For a naive AI with no system prompt about RO, every subsequent turn has to scan/rank ~200 names with prefixes that overlap heavily (tool_route / tool_semantic_route / tool_quick_route / sys_semantic_tool_search; tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity / tool_audit_figure_quality; tool_synthesize / tool_synthesize_plan / tool_synthesis_preview / tool_synthesis_curate_figures). Even with terse `short` strings the listing alone burns ~5-8K tokens. A small / medium AI client will pick wrong because the lexical neighbours are too dense.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Default the MCP list_tools response to ~40 frontline tools (sys_boot, tool_route, sys_help, sys_protocol_get, sys_active_tools, sys_state_get, sys_file_*, sys_path_*, tool_search, tool_synthesize, tool_path_finalize, tool_step_complete, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_research_method, plus the mem_* core 6). Gate the rest behind a `verbose=true` arg or only return them after the AI explicitly calls sys_help / sys_semantic_tool_search.

friction_items.description: list_tools returns a flat list. Nothing in the MCP surface tells a naive AI 'CALL sys_boot FIRST'. sys_help's description starts 'AI orientation — how to use Research OS efficiently' but the AI has no reason to call sys_help before anything else, and tool_route's short description says 'call after every researcher message'. The session-bootstrap sequence is encoded only inside sys_help's BODY which the AI has not yet fetched. Result: a naive AI will pick the first plausibly-named tool (often sys_state_get or sys_workspace_tree) and miss the optimized boot path. Token cost: 3-5K wasted on warm-up calls.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) prepend a `_README` synthetic tool to list_tools whose description is 'CALL ME FIRST — single instruction: call sys_boot then tool_route(prompt=<user message>)', or (b) add a server-side `instructions` field returned in MCP initialize that says the same thing. MCP spec supports server instructions; use them.

friction_items.description: Scenario prompt: 'I have GEO accession GSE12345 and want to do DESeq2 differential expression on it.' Running this through tool_route lexically would lexically hit 'DESeq2' which is listed under guidance/iterative_planning triggers (along with 17 other domain-flavored phrases). That protocol is about 'iterating with the AI on next steps' — NOT about RNA-seq pipeline scaffolding. The naive AI loads iterative_planning, sees `tool_plan_next_step`, calls it, gets a planning markdown about 'options A/B/C' that has zero RNA-seq specifics. Semantic routing might rescue this but only if researcher_config has the semantic extra installed (silent fallback otherwise: method='trigger', confidence='medium').
friction_items.suggested_fix: Move RNA-seq / scRNA-seq / DESeq2 / scanpy / Seurat / Bioconductor triggers OUT of guidance/iterative_planning and into a NEW methodology/bioinformatics_de.yaml protocol (with shortcut to mixed_language_orchestration + external_tool_setup). Until that protocol exists, route these triggers to methodology/pick_tool_stack whose body actually mentions DESeq2/scanpy/Seurat field-practice. Returning a trigger-match against guidance/iterative_planning for a domain method name is a routing bug, not a feature.

friction_items.description: sys_protocol_get defaults to format='full' for back-compat. analysis_plan is ~2K tokens; methodology_selection ~1.8K; pick_tool_stack ~1.5K. A naive AI executing the bio scenario will pull 4-6 full protocols in the first 10 turns (project_startup, analysis_plan, methodology_selection, pick_tool_stack, mixed_language_orchestration, literature_per_step) — 10-15K tokens of protocol prose into context. The description tells the AI to prefer 'summary' first then 'step', but the schema default contradicts that. Naive AIs trust schema defaults more than descriptions.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Change format default to 'summary' for v2.0.0 (MAJOR — flagged as breaking). Update server.py + the default in TOOL_DEFINITIONS. The cost of a few back-compat client breaks at the major bump is far less than the per-session token cost of mis-defaults.

friction_items.description: Naive AI cannot tell, from the MCP surface, that wet_lab pack contains zero bioinformatics analysis content — wet_lab/method/wet_lab_experiment_design will sound to a fresh model like 'the wet-lab biology methodology protocol — use for my bio scenario' when the scenario is dry-lab. There is no signal like `pack_scope: 'bench_only'` or `pack_excludes: ['bioinformatics']`. The AI will fetch wet_lab_experiment_design.yaml, find prose about edge-evaporation gradients and pipetting controls, then have to backtrack. Wasted tokens + wrong tool selection.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `scope_tags` field to every protocol YAML ({domain, audience, exclusions}). tool_route should filter on prompt-context tags (e.g. dry-lab prompts exclude pack='wet_lab'). Also: rename methodology/wet_lab_experiment_design to wet_lab/method/bench_experiment_design (more honest). The current name overpromises.

friction_items.description: RO doctrine: no method-from-training, every choice grounded in a fetched paper. analysis_plan.yaml's ground_methods step has a HARD GATE that BLOCKS synthesis if `tool_search_*` calls weren't logged. A naive AI following the iterative_planning path (which it landed on by lexical misroute, see item 3) may NEVER hit analysis_plan and therefore never know about the gate. It will write a DESeq2 script, conclude, try tool_synthesize, and only THEN discover the literature gate failed — having to backfill literature for every decision retroactively. Failure surfaces at the worst possible moment.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make the literature gate fire EARLIER (at tool_step_complete, not at tool_synthesize). Or: add a `grounding_required: true` advisory to sys_boot's output so a naive AI is warned upfront that synthesis will block without grounded literature. Also: pre-seed grounding records for canonical method papers (DESeq2/Love2014, edgeR/Robinson2010, limma-voom/Law2014, scanpy/Wolf2018, Seurat/Hao2023) so the AI doesn't need a literature search just to cite the obvious.

friction_items.description: analysis_plan/write_atomic_scripts mandates the SUB-TASK PIPELINE pattern (ingest → validate → clean → featurize → split → fit → diagnose → visualize → report) via tool_step_pipeline_define / tool_step_pipeline_run. For a naive AI just trying to run DESeq2 on a counts matrix, this 7-node pipeline scaffold + a separate pipeline.yaml + .pipeline_run audit trail is enormous overhead. The protocol text says 'DO NOT one-shot a 400-line mega-script' — but for the RNA-seq scenario, a 100-line DESeq2 R script IS the right answer and the pipeline-DAG ceremony actively obstructs it. Naive AI either over-complies (over-engineers) or skips and fails the audit.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add an explicit size threshold in the protocol: 'Use the pipeline DAG when the step has ≥3 distinct sub-tasks OR the main script will exceed ~150 LOC. For simple <150-LOC analyses, a single script is fine.' Or: make tool_step_pipeline_define auto-degrade to a no-op (with a friendly comment) for small steps.

friction_items.description: 26 tool_audit_* tools exist (audit_assumptions, audit_citations, audit_claims, audit_cliches, audit_code_quality, audit_coherence, audit_cross_deliverable_consistency, audit_dashboard_content, audit_evalue, audit_figure x5, audit_findings_diff, audit_findings_query, audit_power, audit_prose, audit_quality_full, audit_reproducibility, audit_reviewer_responses, audit_statistical_power, audit_step_completeness, audit_step_literature, audit_synthesis, audit_version_coherence). A naive AI asked 'audit my step' faces a 26-way ambiguity. tool_audit_quality_full is the meta-aggregator but the AI has to KNOW that — its description mentions 6 child tools it runs and 2 it does NOT run. Selecting the wrong audit returns a partial verdict the AI may misinterpret as success.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Collapse the audit surface to 4 user-facing tools: tool_audit (mode={step,synthesis,reproducibility,custom}, with custom taking a list of checks). The 26 internal checkers stay as implementation; the SDK consumer sees a clean menu. Bonus: consolidating tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity / tool_audit_figure_quality into one with `scope` arg.

friction_items.description: Scenario explicitly requires R (Bioconductor DESeq2) + Python (scanpy). methodology/mixed_language_orchestration.yaml is the protocol for this, but the routing entry for it (line ~) lacks RNA-seq-specific triggers — its triggers are vocabulary like 'reticulate' / 'rpy2' / 'mixed language'. A naive AI that knows it needs R + Python won't reach for those words; it will say 'I need to run DESeq2 in R and visualize with scanpy in Python'. Router misses. The handoff scaffold (handoff_plan.md, schema pinning, Parquet vs TSV decision matrix) is GOOD content the AI will not find.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add triggers to mixed_language_orchestration: 'DESeq2', 'edgeR', 'limma', 'scanpy', 'Seurat', 'Bioconductor', 'R and Python', 'cross-language', 'rpy2', 'reticulate', 'R counts to Python AnnData'. Also surface it from pick_tool_stack's decomposition since the two are sister protocols.

friction_items.description: tool_route returns method='semantic' OR 'trigger' with confidence 'high'|'medium'|'low'|'none'. A naive AI does not know what to do when method='trigger' and confidence='medium' — should it accept the route or call tool_semantic_route to double-check? The description doesn't tell the AI when to second-guess. Also: tool_semantic_route requires the `semantic` extra; falls back silently with status='unavailable'. Naive AI sees the status field, doesn't know if that means 'try again' or 'give up'.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `recommended_action` field to tool_route output: 'proceed' | 'confirm_with_user' | 'try_semantic' | 'show_alternatives'. Don't make the AI infer the action from method+confidence — encode it server-side.

friction_items.description: Several tool input schemas have field names that need RO-specific knowledge to populate: `step_id` (in tool_audit_step_literature, tool_step_complete, tool_path_finalize, etc.) is the NN_slug like '01_baseline_eda' — a naive AI will guess just '01' or 'baseline_eda' or the absolute path. Schema descriptions don't standardize how to fetch step_id (it's the dir name returned by sys_path / sys_path_list). Field name conventions are inconsistent: sometimes step_id, sometimes path_name, sometimes slug.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Standardize on one name (`step_id` everywhere) and add description: 'NN_slug like "01_baseline_eda" — see sys_path_list for valid values'. Optionally: tools that take step_id should support step_id='current' as a sentinel resolving to sys_path's current_path.

friction_items.description: project_startup.check_intake_freshness controls whether the AI re-runs intake. A naive AI handed a GEO accession + a half-empty inputs/ folder will hit 'recommended_depth: full' and spend 4-6 turns on intake_autofill + scope_clarification before touching any RNA-seq code. The intake protocol is well-designed but front-loads the friction; a naive AI loses the researcher's trust before producing anything substantive.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Offer a `mode='quick_start'` flag to tool_route. When set, skip intake_freshness gate and jump straight to methodology/pick_tool_stack with a stub intake auto-populated from the prompt. Re-prompt later for any missing required fields. (researcher perspective made a similar suggestion; the naive_ai impact is even worse because the AI doesn't know that backfilling intake is easier than upfront completion.)

friction_items.description: mem_log is the catch-all log call. analysis_plan/ground_methods invokes it three different ways (method+citation, context+selected+rationale, hypothesis+status). The single tool with 8+ optional fields makes the AI guess which fields apply for which call site. Naive AI may omit fields the gate later checks for.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Split mem_log into mem_decision_log, mem_grounding_log, mem_hypothesis_status — three small tools instead of one fat one. The total surface grows by 2 but each tool's schema becomes self-documenting.

friction_items.description: tool_search / tool_literature_search_and_save / tool_research_method / tool_research_tool make real network calls (Semantic Scholar, PubMed, Crossref, etc.) that take 10-30s. MCP tool responses are synchronous — a naive AI may time out its own call or assume the server hung. No streaming progress, no partial-result return.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) return immediate-ack + a poll-id for long calls, OR (b) document expected latency in the tool description so the AI's higher-level orchestrator can set sensible timeouts. Currently no description mentions 'this may take 30s'.

confusing_protocols: guidance/iterative_planning (catches DESeq2/scRNA-seq triggers but body is generic 'next step' planning)
guidance/analysis_plan (~2K tokens; gates fire AT THE END not upfront)
methodology/pick_tool_stack (good content but only reachable via methodology_selection)
methodology/mixed_language_orchestration (essential for this scenario but missing triggers)
recommendations: v2 MAJOR breaking change worth doing: flip sys_protocol_get format default from 'full' to 'summary'. Single biggest token-cost win for naive AI clients.

Add MCP `instructions` field to the initialize handshake: 'On every turn: (1) call sys_boot once per session, (2) call tool_route(prompt=<user_message>), (3) load returned protocol with sys_protocol_get format=summary, (4) call sys_active_tools to scope your working set.' This is the single highest-leverage fix for naive AIs.

Trim default list_tools surface to ~40-60 frontline tools. Gate the rest behind sys_semantic_tool_search or a verbose flag. Splitting by tier ('frontline', 'expert', 'pack', 'adapter') is fine; defaulting to frontline is the win.

Author a methodology/bioinformatics_de.yaml (and methodology/scrnaseq_qc.yaml) protocol scoped for dry-lab DE work — DESeq2/edgeR/limma-voom decision tree, GEO/SRA ingest with checksums, scanpy QC defaults, batch-effect via ComBat-seq / RUVseq / limma::removeBatchEffect, mixed-language handoff for R counts → Python AnnData.

Pre-seed grounding records for canonical bioinformatics method papers (DESeq2/Love2014, edgeR/Robinson2010, limma-voom/Law2014, scanpy/Wolf2018, Seurat/Hao2023, fgsea/Korotkevich2019). Ships with the package so the naive AI doesn't hit grounding-blocker on every canonical choice.

Move RNA-seq / scRNA-seq / DESeq2 / scanpy / Seurat / Bioconductor / GEO / SRA / GSE triggers off guidance/iterative_planning and onto methodology/bioinformatics_de.yaml (once created) + methodology/mixed_language_orchestration + methodology/external_tool_setup. Fix the routing collision.

Consolidate tool_audit_figure_* (5 tools) into one tool_audit_figure with `mode` arg. Consolidate tool_synthesize / tool_synthesize_plan / tool_synthesis_preview / tool_synthesis_curate_figures into one tool_synthesize with `phase` arg.
Add `recommended_action` field to tool_route output instead of making the AI infer from method+confidence. Encode the routing decision server-side.
Add `scope_tags` to every protocol YAML ({domain, audience}) + use them in tool_route to filter wet_lab pack out of dry-lab queries.

Make the literature/grounding gate fire at tool_step_complete (early) instead of only at tool_synthesize (late). Surface 'lit_gate_pending=true' on master audit so naive AIs aren't ambushed mid-synthesis.
Split mem_log into mem_decision_log + mem_grounding_log + mem_hypothesis_status (already partly there with mem_decision_log existing). Removes the multi-modal-args confusion.
Add 'CALL ME FIRST' synthetic _README tool OR populate MCP server `instructions` field with the boot sequence. Eliminates the silent first-turn misroute.
